meta.description: Linux Log Guardian — Turkish-made open-source nginx WAF. log → ban in ~15 min.
hero.title: Linux Log Guardian
hero.tag: nginx access log → WAF/CRS → kernel ban · single chain · self-hosted
about.title: About — Linux Log Guardian
site.purpose: This is a public landing and download page. It serves static content: overview, install guide, evidence pack and contact. Log Guardian does not run here — you install it on your own Linux server.
about.body:
Linux Log Guardian is an open-source (MIT) self-hosted security stack built in Turkey. One chain: nginx log → OWASP CRS → kernel ban.

Full source on GitHub — review, fork and run on your own server.

  • Binary: log-guardian · package: log-guardian_*.deb
  • Core production-ready (~15 min)
  • Dashboard, Grafana, fleet = optional Pro
flow.title: Single chain: log to kernel ban
flow.note: XDR, Wasm marketplace and LLM Copilot are long-term optional layers — Core alone is production-ready.
scope.honest.title: Honest limits (not competing on edge speed)
scope.honest.body:
• Reactive architecture — first request may pass until the log line is parsed; we are not inline ModSec throughput.
  • No L3/L4 DDoS absorb — use CDN/Cloudflare on top.
  • Distributed botnets — per-IP ban; no CrowdSec signal network.
  • We do: log → CRS/WAF → ~17 ms kernel ban, proof PDF, Telegram ops, MIT self-hosted.
install.title: Setup guide (detailed)
install.intro: Fresh Core install on Ubuntu/Debian (~15 min). Source: GitHub — Linux-Log-Guardian. Two paths: .deb (recommended) or build from source. Works without XDP on laptop / VM. In repo: docs/QUICKSTART_NGINX.md · docs/LAPTOP_OPS.md.
install.deb_body:
No compile step. Ships binaries, systemd, rules and scripts. Upgrade-safe: existing /etc/log-guardian/rules.conf is preserved.

Download: GitHub Releases (log-guardian_*_amd64.deb) or build from repo: bash scripts/build_deb.sh → dist/.
install.deb_s1_body: Install Debian package dependencies on first setup. nginx can be added in the same command if missing.
install.block_deb_deps:
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y ipset libbpf1 libcurl4 libpcre2-8-0 libsqlite3-0 \
  libssl3 libelf1 libz1 liburing2 nginx
install.out_deb_deps:
Reading package lists... Done
Setting up ipset (7.x) ...
Setting up libbpf1:amd64 ...
Setting up nginx (1.x) ...
install.deb_s2_body: If dpkg -i reports missing dependencies, run apt-get install -f. postinst creates the log-guardian user, permissions and services automatically.
install.block_deb:
# Copy .deb to server (scp, wget, USB...)
sudo dpkg -i log-guardian_*_amd64.deb
sudo apt-get install -f   # if dependencies missing

# On success:
#   log-guardian kuruldu.
#   sudo bash .../install_first_run.sh
#   bash .../post_install_verify.sh
install.out_deb:
Selecting previously unselected package log-guardian.
(Reading database ... 100%)
Setting up log-guardian (72e5a7b) ...
[deb_post_install] rules.conf olusturuldu: /etc/log-guardian/rules.conf

log-guardian kuruldu.
install.deb_s3_body: Prepares nginx log format, FP trust and API security in one pass. Scripts live under /usr/local/share/log-guardian/scripts/.
install.out_deb_first:
[OK] log-guardian.service active
[OK] log-guardian-daemon.service active
[OK] --health IPC
[OK] API fail-closed (tokensiz 403)

=== ozet ===
  FAIL: 0   WARN: 0
[OK] post_install_verify — kurulum kapisi gecti
install.src_body: Clone from GitHub and build locally. Best for development and full source review.
install.req_body:
• Ubuntu 22.04 / 24.04 or Debian 12 (amd64)
  • nginx with writable access log
  • root/sudo (systemd, ipset, /etc/log-guardian)
  • Optional Pro: Docker (dashboard/Grafana), suitable kernel for eBPF
install.s1_body: Clone, build and run the main installer. install.sh sets up systemd units, rules and nginx log format.
install.block1:
git clone https://github.com/kurtulusutkucenik/Linux-Log-Guardian.git
cd Linux-Log-Guardian
make -j$(nproc)
sudo bash install.sh
install.s2_body: Starts services and prepares API token sync for the dashboard.
install.block_api:
sudo bash scripts/install_first_run.sh
sudo bash scripts/ensure_api_security.sh
bash scripts/sync_dashboard_api_token.sh
install.s3_body: WAF needs the log_guardian format for request body and XFF. Installer usually applies it — verify:
install.block_nginx:
# examples/nginx-log-guardian.conf → site config
STRICT=1 bash scripts/check_nginx_log_format.sh
sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
install.s4_body: Check health, service status and Prometheus metrics. Green gate: post_install_verify must end with FAIL: 0.
install.block_verify:
sudo log-guardian --health
sudo log-guardian --status
# Source install:
bash scripts/post_install_verify.sh
# .deb install:
bash /usr/local/share/log-guardian/scripts/post_install_verify.sh
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:9091/metrics | head
install.out_health:
[HEALTH] daemon IPC: OK
[HEALTH] BPF xdp=OFF execve=OFF lineage=OFF
[HEALTH] RCE det=0 kill=0 lineage=0 connect=0 uptime=…s
install.out_verify:
[OK] log-guardian.service active
[OK] metrics :9091
[OK] API fail-closed (tokensiz 403)
[OK] nginx log_guardian format

=== ozet ===
  FAIL: 0   WARN: 0
[OK] post_install_verify — kurulum kapisi gecti
install.s5_body: Without eBPF/XDP use --no-xdp with ipset bans. If service deps fail, run the repair script.
install.block2:
sudo bash install.sh --no-xdp
sudo bash scripts/install_first_run.sh
# if service FAIL:
sudo bash scripts/repair_no_xdp_stack.sh
install.tip: Tip: JWT setup: bash scripts/laptop_jwt_setup.sh. Quick demo: SKIP_WEBHOOK=1 bash scripts/demo_3min.sh
dashboard.title: Dashboard — after install (optional Pro)
dashboard.where: Important: The dashboard does not run on this marketing site. localhost:3001 is on your own Linux machine where you ran install — a visitor's browser localhost is not your server.
dashboard.body: The Pro dashboard (Next.js): fleet status, ban history, tests and evidence PDFs. Laptop dev: bash scripts/dashboard_dev.sh → http://localhost:3001. Harden with laptop_harden.sh before exposing to the internet.
dashboard.access_row1: Reads this page, downloads source or .deb — does not open port 3001.
dashboard.access_row2: After bash scripts/dashboard_dev.sh on their host, opens http://localhost:3001 on that same machine.
dashboard.access_row3: SSH tunnel: ssh -L 3001:127.0.0.1:3001 … — do not expose with demo password.
dashboard.req_body:
• Core only needs nginx + sudo — dashboard not required
  • Dashboard dev: Node.js + dashboard_dev.sh
  • Grafana additionally grafana_stack.sh (optional)
dashboard.start:
# On the machine where you installed (not this website):
bash scripts/dashboard_dev.sh
# Browser on the same machine:
#   http://localhost:3001
# Login: admin / DASHBOARD_ADMIN_PASSWORD in .env
dashboard.features:
• Fleet: agent online/offline, recent alarms
  • Tests: phase100, competitive-proof, bench
  • Reports: PDF/JSON evidence pack
  • Grafana: tenant metrics via bash scripts/grafana_provision.sh
dashboard.optional: Is Core enough? Yes. If you only need log → WAF → kernel ban, skip the dashboard. CLI (log-guardian --status) and Prometheus (:9091/metrics) ship with Core.
dashboard.preview_note: Screenshots below are examples. Live UI runs only on your own installation.
dashboard.note: Summary: this site = download and learn. Dashboard = on your server, after install, optional.
warn.title: Before exposing to the internet
warn.p2: Public VPS: sudo env LG_NEW_PASSWORD='…' bash scripts/laptop_harden.sh
layers.title: Layers
layers.note: Pro and optional layers are add-ons; Core is enough for day-to-day use.
github.title: Source code (GitHub)
github.intro: Linux Log Guardian is fully open source (MIT). All C source, install scripts, tests and evidence are on GitHub — you install on your own Linux server; this site is only a landing page.
github.steps_body: Clone, build, install; aim for FAIL: 0 in post_install_verify. Step-by-step: Setup · proofs: Tests.
github.contrib: Issues and pull requests welcome. Do not commit API/Telegram secrets — use ensure_api_security.sh and webhook.local.env.example on install.
download.title: Download (.deb package)
download.intro:
Fresh server: setup guide → A) .deb. Package sources:

  • GitHub Releases — log-guardian_*_amd64.deb
  • Source repo — bash scripts/build_deb.sh

Integrity check (no install): bash scripts/test_deb_local.sh
download.verify: Passing extract test: [OK] test_deb_local — extract dogrulama tamam · Real install: sudo dpkg -i dist/log-guardian_*.deb
evidence.title: Evidence pack
tests.title: Validation tests
tests.page_intro: Same matrix as dashboard /tests — install gates, CRS parity, FP, ban latency, corpus recall and 72h soak. Each card is automated script output.
tests.teaser: Same automated test matrix as the dashboard — 28 proofs (install gates, CRS, FP, ban latency, corpus, 72h soak).
tests.intro: Same automated test matrix as the dashboard — install gates (FAIL=0), CRS parity, FP, ban latency, corpus recall and 72h VPS/VM soak. Live installs refresh the same data at dashboard /tests.
tests.note: Raw JSON: competitive-proof.json · PDF: competitive-proof.pdf · Refresh: bash scripts/competitive_proof.sh
laptop.title: Laptop validation matrix
laptop.intro: Laptop: sprint gate + 1h soak. VM/VPS: 72h soak complete (864 samples, 0 failures). Demo: demo_3min.sh.
laptop.block:
SKIP_PHASE100=1 bash scripts/laptop_sprint_gate.sh
SOAK_1H=1 bash scripts/laptop_soak_72h.sh --start
SKIP_WEBHOOK=1 bash scripts/demo_3min.sh
contact.title: Contact & contributions
contact.body: Questions and contributions welcome. Issues and pull requests on GitHub.
footer: Linux Log Guardian · Made in Turkey · MIT · open source
